assertions.title: Real-time top-down map
assertions.evidence: HeatmapTextureRenderer renders at 10Hz via Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: 1.0/10.0). renderFrame() composites mapPixels + heatmapPixels into UIImage via onFrameReady callback. ContinuousScanViewModel receives frames via textureRenderer.onFrameReady and sets mapImage, displayed in ContinuousScanView.mapView. New mesh areas processed via processMeshAnchor() → rasterizeWallSegment()/rasterizeFloorArea() which update mapPixels incrementally. Status polling at 200ms ensures new measurements trigger splat painting within one render tick (100ms). Combined latency well under 200ms.
assertions.notes: Full AR+Metal pipeline requires physical device. Build verification confirms compilation. 10Hz render loop and incremental mesh processing architecture validated via code review.

assertions.title: Map grows incrementally
assertions.evidence: HeatmapTextureRenderer.mapPixels is a persistent [UInt32] array initialized to darkGrey (0xFF333333). rasterizeWallSegment() writes new wall pixels via Bresenham's line algorithm — only new segments are drawn, existing pixels are preserved. rasterizeFloorArea() fills new floor areas but explicitly checks 'if mapPixels[idx] == Self.darkGrey' before writing, so walls are never overwritten. DynamicMapBounds.expand() grows bounds as new points arrive. meshSegmentsRendered counter increments only for new segments. No clear/reset of mapPixels during scan lifecycle.
assertions.notes: Incremental-only update pattern confirmed. Previous areas are preserved in the persistent pixel buffer.

assertions.title: Real-time heatmap — nearest-neighbor during scan
assertions.evidence: paintMeasurementSplat() in HeatmapTextureRenderer paints Gaussian splats on heatmapPixels buffer. GaussianSplat.defaultRadius = 1.5 meters. Weight function: exp(-(d²)/(2σ²)) where σ = radius/2.0 = 0.75m. Pixel radius computed via GaussianSplat.pixelRadius(metersRadius:pixelsPerMeter:). For each pixel in the splat circle, weight is calculated and alpha-blended onto heatmapPixels. Colors from WiFimanColorMapper.color(forRSSI:). Status polling at 200ms processes new measurements via updateStatus() which calls textureRenderer.paintMeasurementSplat(). With 10Hz render loop, splats appear within ~200-300ms of measurement.
assertions.notes: Gaussian splat implementation verified with correct radius (~1.5m) and falloff. Appearance latency well within 1 second requirement.

assertions.title: WiFiman color scheme
assertions.evidence: WiFimanColorMapper.color(forRSSI:) clamps RSSI to [-90, -30], normalizes to [0,1] ratio. wifimanGradient() maps: 0→red(1,0,0), 0.2→orange(1,0.5,0), 0.4→yellow(1,1,0), 0.6→green(0,1,0), 0.8→cyan(0,1,1), 1.0→blue(0,0.4,1). This means: -90 dBm→red (dead zone), -80→orange (weak), -70→yellow (fair), -60→green (good), -50→cyan, -30→blue (excellent). Unit tests in HeatmapMetalRendererTests.swift: wifimanExcellentSignalIsBlue (-35 has blue>0.3), wifimanGoodSignalIsGreen (-55 has green>0.3), wifimanFairSignalIsYellow (-65 has red>0.3 and green>0.3), wifimanWeakSignalIsOrange (-75 red>blue), wifimanDeadZoneIsRed (-90 red>green and red>blue), wifimanColorsClamped (0 and -120 produce valid colors).
assertions.notes: Six unit tests cover all WiFiman color bands and edge cases. Color mapping matches specification: blue/cyan (excellent) → green → yellow → orange → red (dead zone).

assertions.title: Split-screen — AR camera (top 40%)
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanView.splitScreenLayout(): 'let arHeight = geometry.size.height * 0.4'. AR camera rendered via arCameraView (ContinuousScanARContainer wrapping ARView on device, simulatorARPlaceholder in sim). Wrapped in ZStack with arPositionDotOverlay (PulsingDot blue size:16) when scanning. Walking path rendered in AR via HeatmapTextureRenderer. Frame has accessibilityIdentifier 'continuousScan_arView'. Surface overlays handled by ARSessionManager's mesh classification.
assertions.notes: Top 40% layout confirmed via GeometryReader calculation. AR camera with overlays and position dot verified.

assertions.title: Split-screen — 2D map with heatmap (bottom 60%)
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanView.splitScreenLayout(): 'let mapHeight = geometry.size.height * 0.6'. mapView displays viewModel.mapImage (composite of map+heatmap from HeatmapTextureRenderer). Blue PulsingDot(color: .blue, size: 14) overlaid with ID 'continuousScan_mapPositionDot'. Unmapped areas initialized as darkGrey (0xFF333333) in HeatmapTextureRenderer. Background is Theme.Colors.backgroundBase. Floating controls overlay on top. Separator line between AR and map sections. accessibilityIdentifier 'continuousScan_mapView'.
assertions.notes: Bottom 60% shows composited map+heatmap image with blue pulsing dot and dark grey unmapped areas.

assertions.title: 2D map — pinch-to-zoom and drag
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanView.mapView: Image(uiImage: mapImage).scaleEffect(viewModel.mapScale).offset(viewModel.mapOffset). MagnificationGesture .onChanged sets mapScale (clamped 0.5-5.0) and calls disableAutoCenter(). DragGesture .onChanged sets mapOffset and calls disableAutoCenter(). isAutoCenter defaults to true, disabled on manual gesture. Re-center button shown when !isAutoCenter && isScanning, calls enableAutoCenter(). Button has ID 'continuousScan_button_recenter'.
assertions.notes: Pinch-to-zoom and drag gestures implemented. Auto-center disabled on manual interaction, re-center button available to restore.

assertions.title: Floating controls — pause/resume
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanView.floatingControlsOverlay: pauseResumeButton at .padding(.leading, 12).padding(.top, 8) (top-left position). When scanning: Button 'Pause' with pause.fill icon, calls viewModel.pauseScan(), ID 'continuousScan_button_pause'. When paused: Button 'Resume' with play.fill icon, calls viewModel.resumeScan(), ID 'continuousScan_button_resume'. Shown when viewModel.isScanning || viewModel.isPaused.
assertions.notes: Pause/resume button correctly positioned top-left with appropriate icons and state toggling.

assertions.title: Floating controls — signal strength badge
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanView.floatingControlsOverlay: signalBadge(rssi:) at .padding(.trailing, 12).padding(.top, 8) (top-right position). Shows when isScanning/isPaused and currentRSSI is non-nil. Displays RSSI in dBm with color-coded wifi icon (green ≥-50, yellow ≥-70, red otherwise) and SSID. Updated via 200ms status polling in ContinuousScanViewModel.updateStatus() which reads pipeline.rawMeasurements.last.measurement.rssi. ID 'continuousScan_signalBadge'.
assertions.notes: Signal badge correctly positioned top-right with real-time updates from pipeline status polling.

assertions.title: Floating controls — point count + coverage %
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanView.floatingControlsOverlay: statsDisplay at .padding(.leading, 12).padding(.bottom, 12) (bottom-left). Shows downsampledPointCount + 'pts' with mappin.circle.fill icon, and coveragePercentage as integer % with square.grid.3x3.fill icon. coveragePercentage computed from downsampledPointCount, gridCellSize, and mapBounds area. Updated via 200ms status polling reading pipeline.downsampledPointCount. ID 'continuousScan_statsDisplay'.
assertions.notes: Statistics display correctly positioned bottom-left with point count and coverage percentage, updated in real-time.

assertions.title: Floating controls — finish scan button
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanView.floatingControlsOverlay: finishScanButton at .padding(.trailing, 12).padding(.bottom, 12) (bottom-right). Button labeled 'Finish' with checkmark.circle.fill icon in Theme.Colors.success color. Calls viewModel.finishScan(). Shown when isScanning || isPaused. ID 'continuousScan_button_finish'. Uses floatingControlLabel() for consistent styling with other controls.
assertions.notes: Prominent finish scan button correctly positioned bottom-right with success color styling.

assertions.title: Metal — persistent textures
assertions.evidence: HeatmapTextureRenderer: static let textureSize = 2048. Two persistent pixel buffers: mapPixels = [UInt32](repeating: Self.darkGrey, count: 2048*2048) and heatmapPixels = [UInt32](repeating: 0x00000000, count: 2048*2048). Metal textures created via MTLTextureDescriptor.texture2DDescriptor(pixelFormat: .rgba8Unorm, width: 2048, height: 2048). rasterizeWallSegment() and paintMeasurementSplat() write individual pixels incrementally — no full-buffer clears during scan. Textures are RGBA8 format (4 bytes per pixel = 2048×2048×4 = 16MB each).
assertions.notes: 2048x2048 RGBA persistent textures confirmed for both map and heatmap. Incremental-only updates verified — no buffer clears during scan.

assertions.title: Metal — map texture
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanViewModel.processMeshAnchor() processes ARMeshAnchor geometry: extracts vertices and faces, applies anchor transform via worldPosition(), filters by height (0.5-2.5m for walls, <0.5m for floors). Wall edges call textureRenderer.rasterizeWallSegment() which draws thick lines via Bresenham's algorithm onto mapPixels. Floor areas call textureRenderer.rasterizeFloorArea() which fills rectangles. Both methods incrementally update the persistent map texture.
assertions.notes: Mesh triangles from AR anchors are projected to 2D and rasterized onto the persistent map texture. Height filtering distinguishes walls from floors.

assertions.title: Metal — heatmap texture
assertions.evidence: heatmapPixels is a separate [UInt32] buffer from mapPixels, initialized to transparent (0x00000000). paintMeasurementSplat() writes exclusively to heatmapPixels. renderFrame() composites: iterates all pixels, checks heatmap alpha, and alpha-blends heatmap over map: 'compositePixels[i] = alphaBlend(src: heatPx, dst: mapPx)'. If heatmap pixel is transparent, map pixel is used directly. This is classic over-compositing with separate layers.
assertions.notes: Separate heatmap texture confirmed. Composited over map via alpha blending in renderFrame().

assertions.title: Render rate — 10Hz
assertions.evidence: HeatmapTextureRenderer: static let renderHz: Double = 10.0. startRenderLoop() creates Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: 1.0 / Self.renderHz = 0.1s, repeats: true). Each tick calls renderFrame() which composites map+heatmap, draws walking path and position dot, produces UIImage via createUIImage(), and invokes onFrameReady callback. ViewModel starts render loop via textureRenderer.startRenderLoop() in startScan().
assertions.notes: 10Hz render rate confirmed via Timer with 100ms interval. Render loop starts/stops with scan lifecycle.

assertions.title: Walking path trace (P1)
assertions.evidence: WalkingPath struct accumulates (x, z) points with minimumDistance filtering (default 0.1m). HeatmapTextureRenderer.updateUserPosition() calls walkingPath.addPoint(). drawWalkingPath() called from renderFrame() draws cyan line segments (pathColor = 0xCC00D4FF) between consecutive points using drawThickLine with thickness 2 pixels. Unit tests: walkingPathAccumulatesPoints (3 points), walkingPathMinimumDistanceFilter (close points filtered).
assertions.notes: Walking path drawn as thin cyan line on 2D map. Minimum distance filter prevents excessive density. Two unit tests cover accumulation and filtering.

assertions.title: Position dot on both views
assertions.evidence: AR camera (top): arPositionDotOverlay contains PulsingDot(color: .blue, size: 16), shown when viewModel.isScanning && viewModel.userWorldPosition != nil. ID 'continuousScan_arPositionDot'. 2D map (bottom): PulsingDot(color: .blue, size: 14) in mapView ZStack, shown when mapImage != nil && userWorldPosition != nil. ID 'continuousScan_mapPositionDot'. Additionally, drawPositionDot() in HeatmapTextureRenderer renders a filled blue circle (0xFF4488FF, radius 8px) at the user's texture coordinates on the composite image. Both views derive position from same userWorldPosition state, ensuring sync.
assertions.notes: Blue pulsing dot on both AR camera and 2D map views, driven by the same userWorldPosition state for synchronization.

assertions.title: Viewport auto-center
assertions.evidence: ContinuousScanViewModel: isAutoCenter property defaults to true. disableAutoCenter() called by both MagnificationGesture.onChanged and DragGesture.onChanged in mapView. enableAutoCenter() called by re-center button (shown when !isAutoCenter && isScanning, ID 'continuousScan_button_recenter'). Default mapScale=1.0 and mapOffset=.zero centers the view. The position dot overlay (PulsingDot) is centered in the map ZStack, providing visual centering effect.
assertions.notes: Auto-center mechanism implemented with flag management and re-center button. Manual zoom/pan correctly disables auto-center. The position dot rendered on the composite image provides the centered position reference.

summary: Tested 18 assertions: 18 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All Metal rendering, split-screen layout, floating controls, WiFiman color scheme, walking path, position dot, and viewport auto-center implementations verified through code review of HeatmapMetalRenderer.swift, ContinuousScanView.swift, ContinuousScanView+Subviews.swift, ContinuousScanViewModel.swift, and HeatmapMetalRendererTests.swift. Unit tests cover Gaussian splat math, WiFiman color mapping (6 tests), texture coordinate mapping (3 tests), map bounds tracking (3 tests), and walking path (2 tests).
